questions.question: Dispersion is the:
options: Reflection of white light from a plane surface
Splitting of white light into its constituent colors
Bending of light wave around physical obstacles
Combination of different colors to form white light
questions.explanation: Dispersion is the separation of white light into its component colors due to different wavelengths refracting by different amounts.

questions.question: Dispersion occurs in a prism because:
options: Different colors travel at the same speed in glass
Different colors travel at different speeds in glass
The prism reflects different colors at different angles
The prism absorbs some colors and re-emits them
questions.explanation: Each color has a slightly different speed in glass, causing each to refract at a slightly different angle — this is dispersion.

questions.question: When white light passes through a prism, the color that is deviated (bent) the most is:
questions.explanation: Violet light has the shortest wavelength and is slowed the most by glass, so it refracts the most.

questions.question: When white light passes through a prism, the color that is deviated the least is:
questions.explanation: Red light has the longest wavelength and is slowed the least by glass, so it refracts the least.

questions.question: The correct order of colors in the visible spectrum, from longest wavelength to shortest wavelength, is:
options: Violet, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, Red
Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, Violet
Violet, Indigo, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, Red
questions.explanation: ROYGBIV: Red (longest $\lambda$), Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, Violet (shortest $\lambda$).

questions.question: Which of the following has the longest wavelength?
questions.explanation: Radio waves have the longest wavelengths in the EM spectrum — up to kilometers long.

questions.question: Which of the following has the highest frequency?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the highest frequencies (and shortest wavelengths) in the EM spectrum.

questions.question: All electromagnetic waves:
options: Travel at different speeds in a vacuum
Travel at the same speed in a vacuum
questions.explanation: All EM waves travel at $c = 3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$ in a vacuum, regardless of frequency or wavelength.

questions.question: An EM wave has a frequency of $6.0 \times 10^{14}\text{ Hz}$. What is its wavelength in a vacuum? ($c = 3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$)
A radio wave has a wavelength of $300\text{ m}$. What is its frequency? ($c = 3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$)
A natural atmospheric rainbow is formed primarily by the:
options: Specular reflection and scattering of intense sunlight off dense cloud layers.
Refraction, dispersion, and internal reflection of sunlight inside water droplets.
Diffractive wave bending and phase interference of sunlight around falling raindrops.
Continuous thermal refraction and polarized alignment of sunlight through gas columns.
questions.explanation: When white sunlight enters a suspended spherical raindrop, it first refracts (bends) and undergoes dispersion, splitting into its component wavelengths. These colored rays travel through the drop, reflect off the back internal wall, and refract once more as they exit back into the air toward the observer's eyes.

questions.question: Compared to visible light, infrared radiation has:
questions.explanation: Infrared lies just beyond red in the spectrum — longer wavelength and lower frequency than visible light.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered dispersion and the electromagnetic spectrum!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the order of the EM spectrum from radio to gamma rays.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $c = f\lambda$ for EM waves and which colors bend most/least.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: dispersion, ROYGBIV, the EM spectrum order, and $c = 3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$.
